reassignments.rationale: The abstract uses sentinel hospitals to describe acute febrile illness etiology and pathogen frequencies, not platform calibration or extrapolation.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system implementation and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: This is a qualitative evaluation of how primary-care sentinel systems were adapted for COVID-19 surveillance across countries.
The study retrospectively compares CDI incidence and severity trends within a sentinel network rather than calibrating the platform.
This is a retrospective descriptive surveillance analysis with geographic and microbiologic pattern description, not a sentinel-platform methods paper.
The abstract explicitly reports a test-negative design estimating COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness from SARI sentinel surveillance.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance outbreak detection and forecasting
reassignments.rationale: The core method is sentinel time-series monitoring to detect and characterize unusual RSV epidemic timing after NPIs.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system implementation and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: This paper evaluates the informatics and operational implementation needed to run a primary-care sentinel surveillance network.
The method uses negative tests in a sentinel pregnancy population to improve interpretation of case-based STI surveillance denominators and positivity.
Nationwide sentinel sampling is used to estimate JE virus prevalence and risk factors in mosquitoes and bats, which is descriptive surveillance epidemiology.
Sentinel isolates are screened and then characterized with PCR and whole-genome sequencing to study resistance gene dissemination.
The hospitalized-illness study explicitly uses a test-negative design within a multistate sentinel surveillance network.
The paper evaluates whether labor-and-delivery patients can serve as a representative sentinel population for estimating community SARS-CoV-2 prevalence.
Routine PCR testing with sequencing is used as an early-warning genomic sentinel system for Omicron subvariant waves.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system implementation and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: The central contribution is implementation of SARS-CoV-2 testing within existing acute febrile illness surveillance systems.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design, reporting practice, and measurement frameworks
reassignments.rationale: This is a framework-style discussion of how influenza surveillance systems were leveraged for COVID-19 response.
Sentinel syndromic surveillance data are used to estimate hospitalization and mortality risk factors for severe COVID-19.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system implementation and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: The paper describes and evaluates adaptation of Egypt's ARI sentinel surveillance system to COVID-19 requirements.
The new Danish sentinel scheme is used to monitor spatial and temporal epidemiology of C. difficile types.
This is a vaccine adverse-event surveillance study using a self-controlled case series on a national sentinel platform.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system implementation and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: A pilot sentinel system is established to monitor treatment uptake and outcomes for chronic hepatitis B and C.
Long-term sentinel surveillance is paired with genotyping and phylogenetic analysis of enteroviruses.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance outbreak detection and forecasting
reassignments.rationale: The method derives outbreak-start thresholds from sentinel surveillance counts to detect RSV epidemic onset.
Reference-laboratory sentinel surveillance plus enhanced PCR testing is used to detect and characterize an outbreak genomically.
The study calibrates HIV epidemic tracking by comparing sentinel ANC surveillance with a broader PPTCT program dataset.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance estimation and bias correction
reassignments.rationale: Sentinel influenza positivity is combined with hospitalization totals to estimate population influenza-associated hospitalization burden.
A stochastic transmission model is used to evaluate the operating performance of alternative outpatient sentinel surveillance designs.
The paper descriptively analyzes how travel-disease sentinel surveillance changed during the COVID-19 pandemic.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system implementation and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: This is a methodological adaptation of influenza sentinel networks into comprehensive ARI surveillance.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: The abstract explicitly describes an interrupted time series study of antibiotic prescribing using sentinel-network EMR data.
This sentinel surveillance study estimates HIV prevalence and odds ratios for behavioral and incarceration-related risk factors.
The open-cohort sentinel network is used to estimate correlates of gonococcal antimicrobial resistance.
Internet-based self-sampling and mailed DBS testing are evaluated as a complementary surveillance stream outside routine sentinel sites.
The study uses prospective household-level active surveillance to estimate dengue burden beyond passive reporting.
National hospital-based sentinel surveillance is used to describe cholera burden, geography, seasonality, and clinical risk markers.
ANC sentinel surveillance estimates syphilis prevalence and multivariable associations with demographic and obstetric factors.
Nationwide sentinel surveillance is used descriptively to characterize congenital rubella cardiac lesions and mortality patterns.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance outbreak detection and forecasting
reassignments.rationale: The paper evaluates cluster-detection performance and outbreak-response yield from influenza SARI sentinel surveillance.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system implementation and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: A local community-based ophthalmology sentinel system is developed, implemented, and evaluated against surveillance attributes.
The main method is integration of SARS-CoV-2 testing and genomic sequencing into an influenza sentinel network.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system design and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: This paper evaluates representativeness, reporting compliance, and incidence extrapolation for a national STI clinical sentinel system.
The article is a broad review of surveillance strategies and diagnostics rather than an empirical sentinel-platform methods study.
Prospective ICU sentinel surveillance is used to quantify SARI burden, outcomes, and resource use after the pandemic.
This is a systematic review of influenza cost and cost-effectiveness evidence, not a sentinel-surveillance methods paper.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Syndromic surveillance systems and early-warning methods
reassignments.rationale: The project centers on strengthening syndromic sentinel surveillance with sequencing for early detection of new pathogens.
Twice-weekly school PCR screening regardless of symptoms is active surveillance rather than sentinel-platform calibration.
Laboratory-based surveillance with sequence analysis tracks echovirus 11 circulation and variant spread.
PERTINENT uses PCR-positive cases and Bordetella-negative controls in a sentinel hospital network to estimate pertussis VE.
The study calibrates influenza-sentinel SARS-CoV-2 positivity against universal national surveillance data.
Outpatient sentinel and hospital data are used descriptively to characterize atypical RSV seasonality and severity.
Mortuary sentinel surveillance is used to quantify COVID-positive deaths and proximate causes, not to calibrate the platform.
The paper compares cases-per-sentinel with testing and positivity indicators to improve interpretation of influenza activity.
This is a longitudinal testing-based sentinel program with repeated participant testing to estimate incidence trends.
Sentinel ILI/SRI surveillance is used to model risk factors for SARS-CoV-2-associated hospitalization in children.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Infectious disease forecasting from surveillance data
reassignments.rationale: The study compares forecasting models for ILI trends using sentinel surveillance data.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system design and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: This is an explicit retrospective evaluation of HFMD sentinel surveillance performance attributes.
Weekly collections from students regardless of symptoms constitute active cohort surveillance for infection rates and asymptomatic fractions.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system design and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: The paper evaluates a new hospital-laboratory respiratory surveillance platform against WHO mosaic framework objectives.
The abstract explicitly describes a test-negative case-control vaccine-effectiveness analysis embedded in active sentinel surveillance.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system design and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: This is a CDC-guided evaluation of ILI sentinel surveillance timeliness, completeness, sensitivity, and representativeness.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design, reporting practice, and measurement frameworks
reassignments.rationale: The perspective paper sets out an EU/EEA surveillance framework and discusses sentinel-system expansion and design principles.
A statistical model converts age-structured sentinel reports into reconstructed universal case counts after reporting changes.
The contribution is validation of a respiratory phenotyping algorithm and case-identification logic for record-based sentinel surveillance.
Sentinel blood-donor surveillance estimates residual risk and characterizes resistance and clades using sequencing.
The paper compares genome-based sentinel surveillance among asymptomatic testers with national sequence data.
The abstract describes a sentinel-surveillance test-negative study of COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness against hospitalization.
HIV Sentinel Surveillance Plus sequences positive samples to identify circulating subtypes and drug-resistance mutations.
The abstract explicitly reports a test-negative case-control influenza VE study at primary-care and hospital sentinel levels.
Sentinel clinical isolates are characterized with whole-genome sequencing and phylogenetics to track resistance mechanisms.
Hospital sentinel surveillance is used to describe pediatric bacterial pneumonia burden, serotypes, and resistance patterns.
The Bayesian model extrapolates COVID-NET sentinel hospitalization data to national admission rates.
Regional sentinel surveillance data are used descriptively to characterize pathogen circulation during the pandemic.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system implementation and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: The paper implements and evaluates automated online recruitment within a primary-care sentinel surveillance network.
Long-run sentinel surveillance data are used to describe influenza positivity and strain patterns over time.
A multicenter observational sentinel network estimates the effect of early oseltamivir treatment on influenza severity outcomes.
Continuous sentinel surveillance is used to relate venue-network behaviors and other exposures to HIV risk among MSM.
Sentinel and non-sentinel respiratory surveillance are combined to describe HPIV temporal and demographic patterns.
The sentinel study estimates the association between RSV-bacterial coinfection and severity outcomes in hospitalized children.
GAM, catalytic, and TSIR models are used to infer RSV transmission dynamics and climatic drivers from surveillance data.
Identical-sequence pairs from genomic surveillance are used to infer fine-scale spatial and age-structured transmission patterns.
Linear optimization is used to redesign sentinel site allocation by balancing mobility coverage and Indigenous area coverage.
The paper reports interim influenza vaccine-effectiveness estimates from primary-care and hospital sentinel surveillance.
Routine respiratory surveillance detects zoonotic infections and then characterizes viruses with sequencing and antigenic assays.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system design and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: This is a surveillance-attribute evaluation of an integrated ILI/SARI sentinel system after respiratory-virus expansion.
Laboratory-confirmed sentinel data are used to correct clinically defined mumps burden estimates, making case-definition validity central.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system design and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: The paper evaluates influenza sentinel surveillance performance attributes such as data quality, sensitivity, and timeliness.
This is a narrative One Health review of occupational zoonoses surveillance approaches and related public-health gaps.
The ACCESS sentinel clinic network is used descriptively to quantify stages in the hepatitis C care cascade.
Nationwide sentinel surveillance is used to describe RSV epidemiology, subtype circulation, and associated factors.
The sentinel network tracks hepatitis B susceptibility and subsequent vaccination uptake across priority populations.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system design and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: A mixed-method evaluation assesses the validity and reporting performance of Japan's MDRP sentinel surveillance system.
The study develops and tests an ICD-10-based SARI case definition and evaluates completeness and timeliness in register surveillance.
Hospital-based sentinel surveillance combines multiplex RT-qPCR with metagenomic and targeted sequencing for pathogen characterization.
The influenza sentinel platform is compared with national COVID trends and sequencing outputs to evaluate representational utility.
The focus is establishment of sentinel surveillance systems for vaccine safety monitoring during new-vaccine introduction.
SENTINEL and NON-SENTINEL surveillance are used to describe seasonal circulation of influenza and influenza-like viruses.
Primary-care sentinel surveillance is used descriptively to report year-round respiratory viral detections and symptom patterns.
Dogs are treated as a sentinel platform for zoonotic-risk monitoring rather than as a human sentinel case-surveillance design.
This is a scoping review synthesizing empirical evidence on integrating COVID-19 into sentinel surveillance systems.
The study is an explicit multicentre hospital-based test-negative case-control estimate of RSV vaccine effectiveness.
Hospital sentinel ARI surveillance is used to describe pathogen burden and ICU-related risk factors in children.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Sentinel surveillance system design and evaluation
reassignments.rationale: The paper reports assessment results for a respiratory infectious-disease sentinel surveillance system and discusses expansion needs.
STD-clinic sentinel surveillance is used to estimate HIV, syphilis, and hepatitis C burdens and model HIV risk factors.

The central method is an integrated meta-analysis of M. bovis prevalence, with surveillance data used only as validation.
Prospective sentinel surveillance is paired with sequencing to characterize burden, distribution, and viral evolution.
Sentinel dengue data are used to estimate pandemic-period differences in care-seeking delay and hospitalization patterns.
The analysis uses Moran's I and SaTScan to describe spatiotemporal clustering in sentinel Salmonella data rather than endemic-epidemic modeling.
This long-run sentinel study measures RSV burden, seasonality, coinfections, and comorbidity patterns in older adults.
Emergency-department opt-out screening is proposed and evaluated as a low-cost sentinel data stream for hepatitis elimination monitoring.
This is a narrative review synthesizing WNV epidemiology, drivers, and the role of equine sentinel surveillance.
Eight years of sentinel surveillance are used to document emergence patterns and exposures for ceftriaxone-resistant gonorrhoea.
This correction pertains to the same sentinel-data spatiotemporal clustering analysis rather than a different method concept.
Community-based sentinel screening of high-risk groups is used to actively detect suspected Mpox-like disease and trace contacts.
The sentinel platform is used to estimate the association of PCV-10 vaccination with hypoxemia among suspected pneumonia cases.
Integrated sentinel surveillance is coupled with sequencing, phylogenetics, and evolutionary analysis of HMPV over time.
This is a systematic literature review of dengue surveillance tools and outbreak-detection evidence, not a primary syndromic system study.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Syndromic surveillance systems and early-warning methods
reassignments.rationale: Mobile-phone collection of symptom data from formal and informal providers is a direct syndromic surveillance system implementation.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Syndromic surveillance systems and early-warning methods
reassignments.rationale: Cell-phone alerts are used to monitor Ebola suspect cases and deaths through syndrome-based community reporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Syndromic surveillance systems and early-warning methods
reassignments.rationale: HFMD symptom surveillance in ambulatory pediatric practice is used as an outbreak signal, with confirmatory enterovirus testing.
The protocol centers on detecting anomalous gastrointestinal clusters and outbreaks using syndromic and microbiologic streams.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Syndromic surveillance systems and early-warning methods
reassignments.rationale: Laboratory syndromic surveillance of acute febrile or exanthematous symptoms is used to detect underreported arbovirus circulation.
This is a systematic review evaluating the utility of syndromic surveillance for early detection and monitoring of GI infections.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Infectious disease forecasting from surveillance data
reassignments.rationale: The paper's operative method is zero-inflated count-model forecasting of animal outbreak counts from surveillance reports, not a syndromic sentinel design.
